bug: Player felt anchored to a vertical column near the spawn and could only move a limited distance vertically or horizontally.
root_cause: The swim bounds were too small and the out-of-bounds correction pulled the player toward BOUNDS_CENTER. When the player hit a top/side edge, the return vector also dragged them toward the center column.

changes: Expanded SwimBoundsCenter from Vector3.new(0, 32, 35) to Vector3.new(0, 58, 0).
Expanded SwimBoundsSize from Vector3.new(260, 150, 300) to Vector3.new(620, 360, 680).
Replaced center-tether return logic with boundaryReturnVelocity().
boundaryReturnVelocity() pushes only toward the nearest in-bounds point, so crossing the top only pushes down and crossing a side only pushes sideways.
Removed the pre-controller AssemblyLinearVelocity center pull; boundary correction is now blended into the target swim velocity.

device_review_needed: Android: confirm the player no longer feels pulled back to a central vertical line.
Android: hold Up for several seconds and confirm the player can rise across the larger cell volume.
Quest: confirm horizontal and vertical movement are no longer capped near spawn.
